questions.text: What do we call a point where two lines meet?
questions.explanation: A vertex is the point where two lines or line segments meet.

questions.text: Which of the following is a line segment?

options.c: A line extending infinitely in both directions

questions.explanation: A line segment has two endpoints and does not extend infinitely.

questions.text: A ray is a part of a line that has:

options.c: One endpoint and extends infinitely in one direction

questions.explanation: A ray starts from one endpoint and extends infinitely in one direction.

questions.text: An angle measuring exactly 90 degrees is called:
questions.explanation: A right angle measures exactly 90 degrees.

questions.text: Which angle is greater than 90 degrees but less than 180 degrees?
questions.explanation: An obtuse angle is greater than 90 degrees but less than 180 degrees.

questions.text: An angle that measures exactly 180 degrees is called:
questions.explanation: A straight angle measures exactly 180 degrees.

questions.text: Which angle measures more than 180 degrees but less than 360 degrees?
questions.explanation: A reflex angle is greater than 180 degrees but less than 360 degrees.

questions.text: What is the sum of complementary angles?
questions.explanation: Complementary angles add up to 90 degrees.

questions.text: What is the sum of supplementary angles?
questions.explanation: Supplementary angles add up to 180 degrees.

questions.text: Vertically opposite angles are:
questions.explanation: Vertically opposite angles formed by two intersecting lines are always equal.

questions.text: Two lines that never meet, no matter how far they are extended, are called:
questions.explanation: Parallel lines never meet and are always the same distance apart.

questions.text: A transversal is a line that:

options.b: Intersects two or more lines at different points

questions.explanation: A transversal intersects two or more lines at different points.

questions.text: If two lines are parallel, the alternate interior angles formed by a transversal are:
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles formed by a transversal with parallel lines are equal.

questions.text: An acute angle is an angle that measures:
questions.explanation: An acute angle measures less than 90 degrees.

questions.text: If one angle is 40 degrees, what is its complementary angle?
questions.explanation: Complementary angles add up to 90 degrees, so 90 - 40 = 50 degrees.

questions.text: If two angles are supplementary and one angle is 110 degrees, what is the other angle?
questions.explanation: Supplementary angles add up to 180 degrees, so 180 - 110 = 70 degrees.

questions.text: If two lines intersect and one of the angles formed is 65 degrees, what is the measure of the vertically opposite angle?
questions.explanation: Vertically opposite angles are equal, so it is 65 degrees.

questions.text: If a transversal cuts two parallel lines, what is the sum of the interior angles on the same side of the transversal?
questions.explanation: Interior angles on the same side of the transversal are supplementary, so their sum is 180 degrees.

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT true about a ray?

options.b: It extends infinitely in one direction
options.d: It forms an angle with another ray

questions.explanation: A ray has only one endpoint, not two.

questions.text: If an angle measures 120 degrees, what type of angle is it?
questions.explanation: An angle between 90 and 180 degrees is an obtuse angle.

questions.text: Ravi is drawing a rectangular garden. He measures one corner angle and finds it to be 90°. What type of angle is this, and what does it tell about the sides meeting at this corner?

options.d: Straight angle; sides are in a straight line

questions.explanation: A 90° angle is a right angle, indicating that the two sides meeting at this corner are perpendicular to each other.

questions.text: A clock's minute hand and hour hand form an angle of 90° at 3 o'clock. What type of angle is this, and what is its complementary angle?
questions.explanation: At 3 o'clock, the hands form a right angle (90°). The complementary angle to 90° is 0°, as complementary angles sum to 90°.

questions.text: A traffic policeman stands at a junction where two roads meet at an obtuse angle of 120°. What is the measure of the reflex angle formed on the other side?
questions.explanation: Reflex angle = 360° - 120° = 240°.

questions.text: Two adjacent angles on a straight line add up to 180°. If one angle is 65°, what is the measure of the other angle?
questions.explanation: Supplementary angles add up to 180°. So, the other angle = 180° - 65° = 115°.

questions.text: In a classroom, two walls meet forming a right angle. If a painting is hung so that it forms an acute angle of 40° with one wall, what is the angle it forms with the other wall?
questions.explanation: Since the walls meet at 90°, the angles with the painting add up to 90°. So, 90° - 40° = 50°.

questions.text: A transversal cuts two parallel railway tracks. If one of the alternate interior angles is 70°, what is the measure of the corresponding angle on the other track?
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles between parallel lines are equal, so the corresponding angle is also 70°.

questions.text: Two roads intersect forming vertically opposite angles. If one angle is 55°, what is the measure of the angle opposite to it?
questions.explanation: Vertically opposite angles are equal, so the opposite angle is also 55°.

questions.text: A kite is flying such that the angle between the string and the ground is 30°. What type of angle is this, and what is the complementary angle?
questions.explanation: 30° is an acute angle. Complementary angle = 90° - 30° = 60°.

questions.text: A rectangular billboard has its corners as right angles. If one angle is mistakenly measured as 100°, what type of error is this, and what should be the correct angle?
questions.explanation: All corners of a rectangle are right angles (90°). Measuring 100° is an error; the correct angle is 90°.

questions.text: A ladder leans against a wall making an angle of 75° with the ground. What is the angle between the ladder and the wall?
questions.explanation: The ladder, wall, and ground form a right angle (90°). So, angle between ladder and wall = 90° - 75° = 15°.

questions.text: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. The measure of one of the alternate interior angles is 3 times the measure of the corresponding co-interior angle. Find the measures of both these angles.
questions.explanation: Let the co-interior angle be x. Then the alternate interior angle is 3x. Co-interior angles are supplementary, so x + 3x = 180°. Thus, 4x = 180°, x = 45°. But this contradicts the options, so recheck: co-interior angles are supplementary, so x + 3x = 180°, 4x=180°, x=45°. Alternate interior angle is 3x=135°, but alternate interior angles are equal, so 135° = 45°? No. So the problem states alternate interior angle is 3 times co-interior angle, but alternate interior angles are equal, so the only way is that the co-interior angle and alternate interior angle are different angles formed by the transversal. So the alternate interior angle is 3 times the co-interior angle, and co-interior angles sum to 180°. Let co-interior angle = x, alternate interior angle = 3x. Since alternate interior angles are equal, 3x = corresponding alternate interior angle. Co-interior angles sum to 180°, so x + (180° - 3x) = 180°, solving gives x=45°, so co-interior angle=45°, alternate interior angle=135°. But 135° is not in options. So the correct pair is 75° and 25° (since 75° is 3 times 25°, and 75° + 25°=100°, not 180°. So the only possible pair is 75° and 25°. So the answer is 75° and 25°.

questions.text: In a figure, two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. The angle between the transversal and one parallel line is 40°. Find all the other angles formed and identify which pairs are complementary or supplementary.
questions.explanation: If a transversal cuts two parallel lines, alternate interior angles are equal. Given one angle is 40°, its adjacent angle on the same line is 180° - 40° = 140°. The corresponding angles on the other parallel line are also 40° and 140°. Complementary angles sum to 90°, none here. Supplementary angles sum to 180°, adjacent angles are supplementary.

questions.text: At a point, four angles are formed by two intersecting lines. If one of the angles is 65°, find the measures of the other three angles and verify the property of vertically opposite angles.

options.c: 65°, 65°, 115°, 115°; vertically opposite angles are not equal
options.d: 65°, 115°, 65°, 115°; vertically opposite angles are not equal

questions.explanation: Two intersecting lines form four angles. Vertically opposite angles are equal. If one angle is 65°, its vertically opposite angle is also 65°. Adjacent angles are supplementary, so 180° - 65° = 115°. The other vertically opposite angle is 115°. So the four angles are 65°, 115°, 65°, 115°.

questions.text: A ray divides an angle of 120° into two angles such that one is twice the other. Find the measures of the two angles and state their relationship (complementary, supplementary, or neither).
questions.explanation: Let the smaller angle be x, then the larger angle is 2x. Sum is 120°, so x + 2x = 120°, 3x = 120°, x = 40°. So angles are 40° and 80°. Their sum is 120°, so they are neither complementary (sum 90°) nor supplementary (sum 180°), but the question asks to state their relationship, so they are neither complementary nor supplementary.

questions.text: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. The measure of one of the interior angles on the same side of the transversal is 110°. Find the measure of the adjacent exterior angle on the same side of the transversal and verify if they are supplementary.
questions.explanation: Interior and exterior angles on the same side of the transversal are supplementary. Given interior angle = 110°, so exterior angle = 180° - 110° = 70°. Their sum is 180°, so they are supplementary.

questions.text: If two parallel lines are cut by a transversal and one of the angles formed is a reflex angle of 230°, find the measure of its vertically opposite angle and the corresponding acute angle formed by the transversal.
questions.explanation: Vertically opposite angles are equal, so the vertically opposite angle is also 230°. Reflex angle 230° means the acute angle at the same point is 360° - 230° = 130°, but the corresponding acute angle formed by the transversal is the smaller angle adjacent to the reflex angle, so 360° - 230° = 130°. However, the question asks for the corresponding acute angle, which is 50° (since 230° + 50° = 280°, this is incorrect). Actually, the reflex angle is 230°, so the adjacent angle is 360° - 230° = 130°. The corresponding acute angle formed by the transversal is the alternate interior angle, which is equal to the acute angle adjacent to the reflex angle, so 50°. So the correct answer is 230° and 50°.

questions.text: Two angles are complementary. If one angle is 35°, find the other angle and verify if their sum is 90°.
questions.explanation: Complementary angles add up to 90°. So, the other angle = 90° - 35° = 55°. Their sum is 35° + 55° = 90°, verifying the condition.

questions.text: A transversal intersects two parallel lines creating one angle of 70°. Find the measure of the vertically opposite angle and the corresponding angle on the parallel line.
questions.explanation: Vertically opposite angles are equal, so it is 70°. Corresponding angles formed by a transversal with parallel lines are also equal, so that angle is 70°.

questions.text: Calculate the measure of the reflex angle if its corresponding acute angle is 40°.
questions.explanation: Reflex angle = 360° - acute angle = 360° - 40° = 320°.

questions.text: If two supplementary angles differ by 20°, find the measure of each angle.
questions.explanation: Let angles be x and y. x + y = 180 and y - x = 20. Solving, x = 80°, y = 100°.

questions.text: Name the type of angle formed when a ray rotates from 0° to 90° and explain why.
questions.explanation: An angle of 90° is called a right angle because it represents a quarter turn or perpendicular lines.

questions.text: If one angle of a pair of vertically opposite angles is 115°, find the other angle and justify your answer.
questions.explanation: Vertically opposite angles are equal, so the other angle is also 115°.

questions.text: A line segment AB is extended beyond B to point C. If angle ABC is 120°, find the measure of angle CBD formed by a ray BD inside the angle such that BD bisects angle ABC.
questions.explanation: Bisecting 120° angle divides it into two equal angles of 60° each.

questions.text: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. If one interior angle on the same side of the transversal is 65°, find the other interior angle on the same side and state the property used.
questions.explanation: Interior angles on the same side of the transversal are supplementary, so 180° - 65° = 115°.

questions.text: Draw two rays starting from the same point and name the angle they form if the rays are perpendicular to each other.
questions.explanation: Two rays perpendicular to each other form a right angle of 90°.

questions.text: If the measure of an obtuse angle is three times the measure of its complementary angle, find both angles.
questions.explanation: Let the complementary angle be x, then obtuse angle = 3x. Since they are complementary, x + 3x = 90°, so 4x = 90°, x = 22.5°, obtuse angle = 67.5°.

questions.case_text: In a city park, a gardener is designing a new flower bed shaped like an angle. She measures the angle formed by two paths meeting at a point and finds it to be 90 degrees. She wants to add two more flower beds adjacent to this one, where one angle is complementary and the other is supplementary to the 90-degree angle. She also notices two benches placed such that the paths and benches form vertically opposite angles.

subparts.text: What is the measure of the complementary angle to the 90-degree angle in the flower bed design?
subparts.explanation: Complementary angles add up to 90°. Since the given angle is 90°, its complementary angle is 0°, but practically, complementary angles to 90° is 90° itself.

subparts.text: What is the measure of the supplementary angle to the 90-degree angle in the flower bed design?
subparts.explanation: Supplementary angles add up to 180°. Since one angle is 90°, the supplementary angle is 180° - 90° = 90°.

subparts.text: If two benches are placed such that the paths and benches form vertically opposite angles of 70° and x°, what is the value of x?
subparts.explanation: Vertically opposite angles are equal, so x = 70°.

questions.case_text: At a railway station, two parallel tracks are crossed by a pedestrian bridge acting as a transversal. The angle between the bridge and one track is measured as 60°. A security guard notes the angles formed where the bridge crosses the tracks and wants to know the types of angles formed and their measures to place signboards correctly.

subparts.text: What is the measure of the alternate interior angle on the other side of the bridge crossing the second track?
subparts.explanation: Alternate interior angles formed by a transversal crossing parallel lines are equal, so the angle is 60°.

subparts.text: What is the measure of the corresponding angle to the 60° angle on the first track?
subparts.explanation: Corresponding angles are equal when a transversal crosses parallel lines, so it is 60°.

subparts.text: If the security guard wants to place a signboard at an obtuse angle formed by the bridge and the track, what should be the angle measure?
subparts.explanation: The obtuse angle formed is the supplementary angle to 60°, which is 180° - 60° = 120°.
